chapter_context.summary: Ahaz reigns wickedly, practicing child sacrifice; Judah is defeated by Syria and Israel; Ahaz bribes Assyria, shuts temple doors, and dies.

outline.text: Ahaz Reigns Wickedly; Practices Child Sacrifice
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahaz Reigns Wickedly; Practices Child Sacrifice (28:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Judah Defeated by Syria and Israel; Zichri Kills Royal Sons
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Judah Defeated by Syria and Israel; Zichri Kills Royal Sons (28:5-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Oded the Prophet Intervenes; Israeli Soldiers Return Captives
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Oded the Prophet Intervenes; Israeli Soldiers Return Captives (28:9-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Ahaz Plunders Temple to Bribe Tiglath-pileser; Assyria Oppresses Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahaz Plunders Temple to Bribe Tiglath-pileser; Assyria Oppresses Judah (28:16-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

outline.text: Ahaz Sacrifices to Syrian Gods; Shuts Temple Doors; Dies
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ahaz Sacrifices to Syrian Gods; Shuts Temple Doors; Dies (28:22-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Chronicles.

key_themes.title: The Temple Centered Kingdom
key_themes.description: The presence of God's glory in the Temple is the absolute center of national blessing and life.

key_themes.title: Immediate Retribution and Reform
key_themes.description: Good kings who seek the LORD experience immediate victory and peace, while unfaithfulness leads to immediate warning and exile.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 28 prompt you to humble yourself, seek God's face, and turn away from compromise?
What practical reforms can you make in your daily walk to ensure your heart is dedicated to God?

cultural_historical.title: The Levitical Worship System
cultural_historical.text: Unlike the Book of Kings, Chronicles highlights the roles of singers, Levites, and liturgical musicians in the temple's daily administration.
